zh.Halsham, Yorkshire, England: 英國英格蘭越可爾哈爾善姆
zh.Bridlington, Yorkshire, England: 英國越可爾布利德林頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Simon le Constable於1234年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾 易亞斯特 利丁哈爾善姆時，他的父親William Constable of Halsham25歲，母親Cecilia Thweng19歲。他和Katherine Cumberworth至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1294年3月1日之前在英國越可爾，埋葬在英國越可爾隆索普。

zh-hans.Bridlington, Yorkshire, England: 英国越可爾布利德林頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Simon le Constable于1234年出生在英国英格兰越可爾 易亞斯特 利丁哈爾善姆时，他的父亲William Constable of Halsham25岁，母亲Cecilia Thweng19岁。他和Katherine Cumberworth至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1294年3月1日之前在英国越可爾，埋葬在英国越可爾隆索普。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Constable
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southeastern): occupational name for the law-enforcement officer of a parish, from Middle English, Old French conestable, cunestable, from Late Latin comes stabuli ‘officer of the stable’. The title was also borne by various other officials during the Middle Ages, including the chief officer of a household or court, or a military officer, and this may in some cases be the source of the surname.
Americanized form of Dutch Constapel, an occupational name for the chief gunner aboard a ship or in the garrison of a fort.

variants.name: Costabile
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Aldo, Antonio, Ciro, Nino, Agnese, Angelo, Duilio, Enrico, Ercole, Franco, Palma.
occupational name from medieval Greek kontostablēs ‘chamberlain’, from medieval Latin comes stabuli, literally ‘count of the stable’ (see Constable ).

nameMeanings.name: Simon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

variants.name: Simen
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Simon le Constable was born in 1234, in Halsham, Yorkshire East Riding, England, United Kingdom, his father, William Constable of Halsham, was 25 and his mother, Cecilia Thweng, was 19. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Katherine Cumberworth. He died before 1 March 1294, in Yorkshire, England, and was buried in Longthorpe, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Simon le Constable est né en 1234, à Halsham, Yorkshire East Riding, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Constable of Halsham, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Cecilia Thweng, avait 19 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Katherine Cumberworth. Il est décédé avant 1 mars 1294, à Everwyck, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Longthorpe, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Simon le Constable 1234 in Halsham, Yorkshire East Riding, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Constable of Halsham 25 und seine Mutter Cecilia Thweng 19. Er hatte mit Katherine Cumberworth mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb vor 1. März 1294 in Yorkshire, England und wurde in Longthorpe, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Simon le Constable è nato il nel 1234 nel Halsham, Yorkshire East Riding, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William Constable of Halsham, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Cecilia Thweng, aveva 19 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Katherine Cumberworth. Morì prima del 1 marzo 1294, nel Yorkshire, England, e fu sepolto a Longthorpe, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Simon le Constableが1234年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイア エアスト リディングハルシャムで生まれた時，父William Constable of Halshamは25歳，母Cecilia Thwengは19歳であった。彼はKatherine Cumberworthとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1294年3月1日以前にイギリスヨークシュアイアで死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアロンソルペにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Simon le Constable(이)가 1234년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 할샤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Constable of Halsham(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Cecilia Thweng(은)는 19세였다. 그는 Katherine Cumberworth(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1294년 3월 1일 이전 영국 요르크실르에서 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 론그소에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Simon le Constable nasceu em 1234, em Halsham, Yorkshire East Riding, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William Constable of Halsham, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Cecilia Thweng, tinha 19 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Katherine Cumberworth. Ele faleceu antes 1 de março de 1294, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, e foi sepultado em Longthorpe, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Simon le Constable родился в 1234 г. в местности холшам, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William Constable of Halsham было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Cecilia Thweng, 19. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Katherine Cumberworth. Он умер перед 1 марта 1294 г. в местности еркшир, Англия и был похоронен в местности лонгтаурп, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Simon le Constable nació en 1234, en Halsham, Yorkshire East Riding, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William Constable of Halsham, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Cecilia Thweng, tenía 19 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Katherine Cumberworth. Murió antes de 1 de marzo de 1294, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, y fue sepultado en Longthorpe, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
